mnemonics.title: Scheduling Criteria
mnemonics.content: C UT WR - CPU Utilization, Throughput, Turnaround time, Waiting time, Response time - SAYS UTENSILS WHILE REACTING - UTENSILS is key word

mnemonics.title: Thread Models
mnemonics.content: MOM - Many-to-One, One-to-One, Many-to-Many - THINK OF FAMILY RELATIONSHIPS: MANY KIDS ONE MOM, ONE KID ONE MOM, MANY KIDS FEW MOMS

acronyms.usage: Selects shortest burst time, minimizes waiting
Time quantum based, fair sharing, circular queue
All CPUs equal, common queue or per-CPU queues
Thread managed in user space, fast but cannot block

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a CIRCULARcarousel with horses (threads). Each horse gets one ride (time quantum), then moves to back of line.
Think of restaurant orders - chef always first to minimize overall cooks shortest dish wait time for all customers.
Process = entire book being swapped, Thread = just bookmark moved. Much faster!
Many elevator buttons (user threads) but only one elevator shaft (kernel thread) - if shaft busy, all buttons wait.

analogies.analogy: Like a teacher managing student presentations - decides who presents next, for how long, and when to interrupt.
Like taking turns in a board game - each player gets fixed time to make moves, then passes to next person.
Like airport security lines - VIP/emergency passengers get faster processing despite arrival order.
Like switching between tabs in browser - saves state of current task, loads state of new task, but faster than closing and reopening.
Like hospital triage - critical patients go to immediate care, non-urgent to regular queue, each with different scheduling rules.
